Tehran FIR effectively closed -- Imam Khomeini International shut for civilian ops, only state/medical/emergency flights permitted

Israel lifted all wartime restrictions on air operations April 9
US-Iran delegations meeting in Pakistan April 11 -- Trump said 'optimistic' about potential deal

fuelOil.summary: Brent is holding around $96-98 after the April 7-8 US-Iran ceasefire announcement triggered a 13-15% one-day crash. Prices down ~10% on the week despite Friday bounce on ceasefire wobbles (Israel-Lebanon strikes, continued Hormuz blockage). Goldman Sachs cut forecasts. IATA jet fuel monitor still lagging spot; next weekly release April 13.
details: Brent ~$97.78/bbl at 9am ET April 10 (Fortune) -- up $4.02 vs prior day after intraday plunge and rebound
Brent at $96/bbl on April 9 (CNBC) -- ceasefire whiplash keeps risk premium elevated
Crashed 13-15% on April 7-8 from $113-117 to $95.80 -- biggest one-day drop since 1991 Gulf War
Weekly decline tracking ~10%+ despite Friday rebound
Goldman Sachs cut oil price forecasts after ceasefire announcement
Goldman warning: another month of Hormuz closure would keep Brent above $100 throughout 2026
IATA jet fuel monitor last reading $195.19/bbl (April 6, pre-ceasefire) -- next release April 13
Jet fuel still trending high but spot should correct downward over coming 1-2 weeks if ceasefire holds
AirAsia X 30-40% fare hikes and 20-25% fuel surcharges (April 6) still not fully rolled back
China and Thailand fuel export bans still in effect -- no lifting announced
Vietnam supply chain still extended; 50+ day backlog unresolved
IATA's pre-war projection was $88/bbl for 2026 -- current level roughly $10 above baseline

geopolitical.iranConflict: Two-week US-Iran ceasefire framework (announced April 7-8, Pakistan brokered) is holding but fragile. US and Iranian delegations meeting in Pakistan April 11. Iraq, Syria, and Bahrain reopened airspace; Israel lifted wartime air restrictions April 9. But Tehran FIR still effectively closed, Kuwait still closed, Strait of Hormuz still blocked for shipping, and Iran reclosed the Strait April 9 in response to Israeli strikes on Lebanon. Trump called himself 'optimistic'; framework expires ~April 21-22.
airspaceClosures: Iran (OIIX FIR) -- Tehran/Imam Khomeini effectively CLOSED to civilian traffic (state/medical/emergency only)
Kuwait airspace -- still CLOSED despite ceasefire
Israel (LLLL FIR) -- wartime restrictions LIFTED April 9; normal operations resuming
UAE, Qatar -- operations resuming but still at reduced capacity
geopolitical.regionalStability: Southeast Asia airspace stable and unaffected by Middle East. Regional fuel export bans (Thailand, China) still in effect -- no lifting announced. Vietnam supply chain still constrained. Ceasefire has begun easing corridor access for Asia-Europe routes (Iraq/Syria open again) but full normalization remains 1-2 weeks out if framework holds.
details: Ceasefire announced April 7 evening, formalized April 8 (Al Jazeera, CBS, Wikipedia)
Duration: two-week framework, expiring ~April 21-22 -- NOT a permanent deal
Iraq, Syria, Bahrain reopened airspace April 8-9 (ch-aviation, Wego)
Israel lifted all wartime air operation restrictions April 9
Tehran FIR still closed per NOTAM; only state/medical/emergency flights permitted
Kuwait airspace still fully closed
Iran reclosed Strait of Hormuz April 9 in response to Israeli strikes on Lebanon (Euronews)
April 9: NPR reports 'fragile ceasefire shows cracks' as attacks continue across the region
April 11: US-Iran delegations meeting in Pakistan; Israel agreed to talks with Lebanon
EASA Conflict Zone advisory still active through April 10; renewal expected
If ceasefire collapses before April 21, BKK-CPH risk reverts to HIGH within 24h

airasia.cancellationRate: Elevated but stable. Thai AirAsia frequency cuts to 13 destinations still in place. AirAsia X 10% flight cuts still active. CXR-DMK FD647 still operating daily as of April 10.
airasia.financialHealth: AirAsia X no fuel hedging. Q1 affiliate losses exceeded $180M. April 6 measures (30-40% fare hikes, 20-25% fuel surcharge, 10% network cut) not yet rolled back. Brent drop and partial airspace reopening will ease pressure if sustained.
airasia.recentDisruptions: Thai AirAsia FD647 CXR-DMK still operating daily. Thai AirAsia frequency cuts to 13 destinations still in place. AirAsia X 10% network cut still in effect. Songkran adds DMK congestion April 13-15 -- elevated delay risk.

thaiAirways.cancellationRate: Near normal for rerouted operations. BKK-CPH prior cancellations announced through April 30; schedule restoration post-ceasefire still pending confirmation.
thaiAirways.financialHealth: Exited bankruptcy June 2025 as privatized public company. Government-backed. 10-15% ticket price increases still in place. Brent crash and corridor reopenings will ease pressure if sustained.
thaiAirways.recentDisruptions: BKK-CPH operating via rerouting. Southern corridor now more viable as Iraq/Syria/Bahrain airspace reopened April 8-9. Still adds 90min-3hrs vs pre-war. Confirm flight status ~April 15-20 as schedule firms up.

cxrToDmk.notes: SEA airspace unaffected by Middle East closures. Primary risks at 2 days out: Thai AirAsia operational reliability, Songkran congestion at DMK (April 13-15 peak), Vietnam jet fuel supply. FD647 still operating daily but verify in AirAsia app today. Backup: VietJet or Vietnam Airlines CXR-BKK -- but booking window effectively closed.

bkkToCph.notes: Corridor access improving: Iraq, Syria, Bahrain reopened April 8-9. Israel lifted wartime restrictions April 9. Tehran and Kuwait still closed. Thai Airways still rerouting via southern (Egypt-Saudi-Oman) or northern (Caucasus) corridor -- adds 90min-3hrs. Expect 13-15hr flight instead of 11-12hr. If ceasefire holds through April 21, further reopenings likely before April 27 departure.

actions.action: Open AirAsia app right now and verify FD647 CXR-DMK April 13 is still scheduled and not delayed. At 2 days out this is the last realistic checkpoint. Screenshot the booking. If any disruption signal appears, call AirAsia Thailand support directly -- online rebooking window is essentially closed.

Track ceasefire status daily through April 21. Iran-US delegations meeting in Pakistan April 11 is the immediate tell. If ceasefire collapses, BKK-CPH rerouting may worsen and risk reverts to HIGH within 24h. Watch CNBC, Reuters, Al Jazeera, and safeairspace.net.

On April 15 check Thai Airways BKK-CPH April 27 booking on thaiairways.com. Prior cancellations were announced through April 30; confirm your flight isn't on that list and schedule has firmed up. Expect still 90min-3hrs longer than originally booked due to rerouting.
Pack for Songkran delays at DMK April 13. Allow at minimum 90 extra minutes connection/transit buffer. Songkran festival is Apr 13-15 -- DMK will be at peak congestion on arrival day.

oilPriceTimeline.event: Pre-conflict baseline. Markets calm, IATA forecast $88/bbl for 2026.
Brent drops to $67.4 as US-Iran diplomatic talks ease tensions.
Markets turn bullish anticipating US military action against Iran. Brent trading ~$10 above fair value.
Last day before strikes. Oil at pre-war levels.
US-Israeli strikes on Iranian military targets. War begins. Immediate price spike.
Iranian missile/drone retaliation. Brent surges 10-13% from pre-war.
Iranian drones strike Nakhchivan airport (Azerbaijan). Brent up 15% since war began.
Thailand bans fuel exports. China restricts refined oil exports.
Peak spike. Near halt in Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic. 65% increase from Feb 27.
Partial correction. Still up 37% from pre-war. IATA warns of sudden price challenge.
IATA weekly monitor: jet fuel $197/bbl for week ending Mar 20, up 58.4% from Feb 20.
Brent eases but jet fuel stays elevated due to supply chain lag.
Iran rejects US peace talks. Prices reignite. Jet fuel up 12.6% week-on-week.
Brent closes at highest since July 2022, up 4.2% on day.
Brent at 4-year highs. Jet fuel peaks at $234.3/bbl. Vietnamese airline cuts take effect April 1. Goldman: $14-18 geopolitical risk premium.
SHARP REVERSAL. Trump says war could end in 2-3 weeks. Brent drops $12/bbl (-10.3%). Kuwait airport struck by Iranian drones.
DE-ESCALATION FAILS TO HOLD. Brent reverses back to $112.42 (+11.2%). IATA jet fuel avg: $195.19 (down 0.9%).
Slight easing. AirAsia X declares 'real crisis', raises fares 30-40%, cuts 10% of flights. Trump escalates rhetoric then pivots.

MAJOR DE-ESCALATION. Trump announced 2-week US-Iran ceasefire framework April 7 evening (Pakistan brokered). Iran agreed to safe passage through Strait of Hormuz. Brent crashed 13-15% from $113-117 intraday to ~$95.80 -- biggest one-day drop since 1991 Gulf War.
Ceasefire cracks. Iran reclosed Strait of Hormuz in response to Israeli strikes on Lebanon. Brent steadies around $96. Iraq/Syria/Bahrain airspaces reopened. Israel lifted wartime air restrictions.
Brent up $4.02 intraday on ceasefire whiplash (plunge then rebound over Hormuz doubts). Goldman Sachs cut oil price forecasts. Week-over-week still down ~10%.
US-Iran delegations meeting in Pakistan. Trump 'optimistic' about potential deal. Israel agreed to talks with Lebanon. Ceasefire framework holding but fragile.

cancellationTimeline.details: Normal disruption levels across SEA. Typical daily: 50-100 delays, <20 cancellations region-wide.
1,198 delays + 21 cancellations across Changi, Jakarta, Bali, Manila, Cebu. Pre-war disruptions.
4,216 delays + 62 cancellations. AirAsia alone had 247 delays.
Disruptions across Thailand, Japan, Indonesia, Vietnam, Philippines, Hong Kong.
1,400+ delays + 93 cancellations. Jakarta: 104 delays, KL: 470 delays.
WAR BEGINS. 2,513 cancellations + 2,117 delays. Changi: 32 cancelled. BKK: 32 cancelled.
150-300% above normal. JAL, Thai Airways, Air India, ANA, AirAsia, Lion Air all affected.
328 Thailand flights disrupted. Hundreds stranded across Asia.
1,043 delays + 184 cancellations across SEA. AirAsia among hardest-hit.
AeroThai: cumulative 1,000+ flights cancelled in Thailand alone due to Gulf crisis.
65+ international/regional flights cancelled across Indonesia, Malaysia, Hong Kong, Taiwan.
325 delays + 50 cancellations in Thailand. Thai Airways, Bangkok Airways, AirAsia, Emirates affected.
Vietnamese airlines announce coordinated pullback from April 1.
Vietnamese airline cuts active. 5,993 delays + 278 cancellations across 19 Asian airports. Kuwait airport drone strike.
Thai AirAsia frequency cuts to 13 destinations confirmed. AirAsia X cancelled daily DMK-Shanghai Pudong. SAS cancelled 1,000 European flights.
AirAsia 116 group delays. AirAsia X officially cuts 10% of flights, fares up 30-40%.
Ceasefire announced April 7 evening; operational impact not yet materialized. CXR-DMK FD647 still operating.

Iraq, Syria, Bahrain reopened airspace. Israel lifted wartime restrictions. Kuwait and Tehran still closed. Asia-Europe corridors incrementally easing. SEA disruption pattern still elevated but stabilizing.

US-Iran delegations meeting in Pakistan. Thai AirAsia FD647 CXR-DMK still operating daily. AirAsia X 10% cuts still in place. Thai Airways BKK-CPH still rerouted but corridor access improved. Songkran surge capacity active in Thailand (191 extra domestic round-trips).

seaAirlineSummary: Ceasefire is holding but fragile (April 11). Brent stabilized ~$96-98 after the April 7-8 crash; week-over-week down ~10%. Iraq, Syria, Bahrain reopened airspace April 8-9 and Israel lifted wartime restrictions -- Asia-Europe rerouting corridors improving materially. Tehran and Kuwait still closed; Strait of Hormuz still blocked for shipping. Operational picture in SEA: Thai AirAsia FD647 CXR-DMK still operating daily, Thai Airways BKK-CPH still rerouted via alternate corridors. AirAsia X 10% network cut and 30-40% fare hikes not yet rolled back. CAAT added 191 extra domestic round-trips for Songkran (April 10-15), so Thai-side capacity is well supported on flight day. Key unknown: whether the two-week ceasefire framework survives past April 21 -- if it collapses, risk reverts to HIGH within 24h.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Vietnam Airlines
seaAirlineOverview.impact: April 1 cuts still active: 7 domestic routes suspended, 23 weekly flights cut. Ceasefire too recent to drive restoration.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Imports 70% of jet fuel, 60%+ from Thailand and China -- both still maintaining export bans. Fuel delivery timelines still extended at 50+ days.

seaAirlineOverview.name: VietJet Air
seaAirlineOverview.impact: 8% regional network reduction still active. Korea-Vietnam routes cancelled including some Nha Trang flights.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Budget model highly vulnerable. Brent stabilization to ~$96-98 easing pressure if sustained.

seaAirlineOverview.name: AirAsia (Group)
seaAirlineOverview.impact: AirAsia X: 10% flight cuts, fares up 30-40%, fuel surcharge 20-25% (April 6) -- not yet rolled back. Thai AirAsia frequency cuts to 13 destinations still active.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: No fuel hedging. Thai AirAsia still operating CXR-DMK daily (FD647) as of April 10. Recovery from April 6 measures expected to lag ceasefire by 1-2 weeks.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Thai Airways
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Rerouting European services via southern or northern corridor. Corridor options improving after Iraq/Syria/Bahrain reopening. BKK-CPH still operating.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Exited bankruptcy June 2025. Government-backed. 10-15% ticket price increases still in place. Prior BKK-CPH cancellations through April 30 -- schedule confirmation pending.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Cebu Pacific
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Major route suspensions: Davao-BKK, Iloilo-BKK, Iloilo-SGN, Clark-HAN all suspended Apr-Oct 2026.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Philippines fuel emergency still in effect.

seaAirlineOverview.name: SAS
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Cancelled 1,000 flights in April 2026 citing fuel crisis. Focusing on short-haul routes within Norway.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: European carriers responding aggressively. Relevant context for CPH arrivals.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Bangkok Airways
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Fuel surcharges of 1,200-2,000 baht. No major route suspensions reported.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Boutique positioning helps retain premium passengers.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Nok Air
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Fuel surcharges in effect. Domestic Thai routes under pressure. Participating in Songkran capacity boost.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Low-cost model. Operating on thin margins.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Lion Air
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Part of broader disruption pattern. Indonesian domestic market under fuel pressure.

broaderContext.narrative: April 11 update: Ceasefire framework is holding at the 3-4 day mark but fragile. Iraq, Syria, and Bahrain reopened airspace April 8-9; Israel lifted wartime air restrictions April 9 -- a meaningful improvement in Asia-Europe corridor access. But Tehran and Kuwait remain closed, and Iran reclosed the Strait of Hormuz April 9 in response to Israeli strikes on Lebanon. US-Iran delegations are meeting in Pakistan today; Trump said 'optimistic' about a potential deal. Brent stabilized around $96-98 after its April 7-8 crash, down ~10% week-over-week. For Svo's window: CXR-DMK (2 days out) is primarily an AirAsia-operational + Songkran-congestion play now -- ceasefire is a background factor, not a direct driver. BKK-CPH (16 days out) is still the main risk; it lives or dies on whether the ceasefire framework survives past April 21. If it holds and extends, corridor reopenings should cascade further before departure. If it collapses, risk reverts to HIGH within 24h.

policies.description: PM Order 2/2026: Banned exports of gasoline, gasohol, diesel, Jet A-1 aviation fuel, and LPG (except to Cambodia and Laos). Still in effect.
Banned refined fuel exports, including jet fuel. Still in effect.
Declared fuel emergency. 4-day government work week.
Coordinated airline pullback in effect. Vietnam Airlines: 7 domestic routes suspended. VietJet: 8% regional reduction.
Military junta introduced odds-and-evens driving system (fuel rationing).
Two-week US-Iran ceasefire framework announced, Pakistan brokered. Delegations met in Pakistan April 11. Framework expires ~April 21-22.
Songkran capacity boost: 6 airlines added 191 round-trip domestic flights (29,685 extra seats) and cut fares 15-30% on 11 routes.

broaderContext.outlook: Elevated but trending down -- fully contingent on ceasefire holding. Key watch dates: April 11 (Pakistan talks), April 12 (final CXR-DMK verification), April 13 (AirAsia CXR-DMK + Songkran peak), April 21-22 (ceasefire expiry -- less than a week before BKK-CPH), April 27 (Thai Airways BKK-CPH). If ceasefire holds and extends, expect further corridor reopenings (Tehran, Kuwait) and IATA jet fuel monitor to drop toward $160-170/bbl by mid-to-late April.
